features.summary: Johnson & Johnson reported fourth quarter 2021 worldwide sales of $24.8 billion, a 10.4% increase year-over-year. Net earnings for the quarter were $4.7 billion, with diluted earnings per share rising to $1.77 from $0.65 a year ago. For the full year, consolidated sales reached $93.8 billion, up 13.6%, and adjusted earnings per share increased by 22% to $9.80.
features.management_outlook: Management anticipates operational sales growth for 2022 between 7.0% and 8.5%, with adjusted earnings per share projected in the range of $10.60 to $10.80. The company is focused on the successful separation of its Consumer Health business and expects to maintain strong performance in Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices, despite ongoing challenges from COVID-19 and inflationary pressures.
features.risks: Key risks include the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, supply chain constraints, and inflation affecting input costs. Additionally, the anticipated loss of exclusivity for STELARA in September 2023 poses a risk to revenue, as biosimilars may enter the market, potentially impacting sales.
features.key_drivers: Sales growth was driven by strong performance in the Pharmaceuticals segment, particularly in immunology and oncology, as well as recovery in Medical Devices. The Consumer Health segment faced challenges due to supply constraints but saw growth in over-the-counter products. Continued investment in R&D and strategic acquisitions are expected to support long-term growth.
